title: CORS error due to missing or misconfigured header
symptom: When making a request from a web app, the browser blocks the request due to CORS policy, indicating that the 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header is missing or incorrectly set.
applies_when: Making requests to n8n webhook from a different origin
CORS settings are configured in n8n
not_this_if: CORS headers are correctly set and the request is from the same origin
root_cause: The CORS error occurs because the server response does not include the 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header, or it is incorrectly configured to allow multiple origins, which is not permitted. This prevents the browser from allowing the web app to access the resource.
fix_steps: Open your n8n .env file or the environment settings where n8n is configured.
Set the following values: N8N_DEFAULT_CORS=TRUE and N8N_CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN=https://mywebsite.co.uk.
Restart n8n to apply the changes.
If using NGINX, add the appropriate CORS headers in the NGINX configuration and reload NGINX.
agent_instruction: Configure CORS settings in the n8n .env file and restart n8n.
